description: Discovery template: the assistant fills these fields from user answers. Scenes (max 5) and characters.cast (max 3) are saved as named versions when the user confirms both; approve merges the selected version into the stored brief.

brand.name: String — Brand name as it should appear on screen
brand.tagline: String — Brand or campaign tagline, if any

product.name: String — Product name as it should appear on screen
product.usp: String — The ONE thing this ad communicates
images: URL — High-res product images (lifestyle or packshot)

creative_direction.theme: String — Creative theme (e.g. high-energy fitness, minimal luxury unboxing, raw UGC testimonial)
creative_direction.mood: String — Emotional atmosphere (e.g. empowering, cozy, tense, celebratory)

the_hook.type: Enum [visual_surprise, bold_claim, question, product_in_action, pattern_interrupt, relatable_moment, sound_hook, text_hook] — Hook mechanic
the_hook.visual: String — What the viewer SEES in seconds 0.0–1.5
the_hook.audio: String — What the viewer HEARS in seconds 0.0–1.5

characters.talent_type: Enum [real_actor, ai_avatar, animated_character, ugc_style_creator, hand_model, no_talent]

cast.role: String — e.g. Main presenter, Product user

scenes.action_description: String — What happens during this scene: character actions, product interactions, narrative beat, and emotional arc. Describe the story, not just the visual.
scenes.start_frame_description: String — Detailed visual description of the OPENING composition: camera angle, shot size, lighting, setting, character positions, product placement. This becomes the start keyframe.
scenes.end_frame_description: String — The AFTER state: what the scene looks like after the action has played out. Must show narrative progression from the start frame, not just a camera change. This becomes the end keyframe.
scenes.camera_movement: String — Camera motion across the whole shot (e.g. 'slow dolly in', 'pan left to right', 'crane up', 'handheld tracking', 'static', 'push in to close-up', 'pull back to reveal')

dialogue.speaker: String — Who speaks: character role (e.g. 'protagonist', 'barista') or 'voiceover'. Omit dialogue object entirely if the scene has no speech.
dialogue.delivery_note: String — Tone/emotion/pacing (e.g. 'whispered, intimate', 'excited, upbeat', 'calm and authoritative')
